hypothesis: The autonomous fringe and whole-word transducer form one exact moving strip, and the universal rational nearest-neighbor coupled additive cocycle class can be decided completely.
interpretation: The right fringe and schedule survivor are exactly the low and high halves of one moving right-edge Rule 30 row, and the reversed inverse word supplies a local width-growing transfer across that strip. The complete nearest-neighbor rational additive ansatz collapses to word length with constant fringe-head and terminal-pair potentials, so it cannot distinguish the branch or a zero output pair.
limitations: the cocycle theorem covers only universal rational nearest-neighbor additive functionals
an invariant restricted to the unique actual fringe orbit may evade the local-head classification
nonlinear and growing-memory coupled invariants remain open
does not prove the alternating survivor has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: The moving-cut decomposition, coupled strip transfer, and nearest-neighbor rational cocycle classification are all-width/all-word statements proved in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_coupled_strip.md. The analyzer independently checks 65 moving cuts, 5,824 transfer cases, and the exact rank-18 linear system.
question: problem1

complete_solution.boundary_sensitivity: the increment cannot distinguish q=t from q=u or any terminal pair
complete_solution.word_functional: F(H)=c*len(H), up to an identically-zero nearest-neighbor coboundary

coupled_transfer.exact_fringe_update: A_(m+1) is the autonomous two-step Rule 30 fringe map

exact_conclusions.finite_shadow_cut: the truncated seed evolves to the same fringe low half and the ordinary inverse-word state as its high half
exact_conclusions.moving_cut: the reversed right fringe and future survivor are the exact low/high halves of one moving right-edge row
exact_conclusions.range_two_no_go: no universal nearest-neighbor additive cocycle on the coupled head/word tiles distinguishes the fringe branch or reconstructed pair
